role_text: You are an energy system maintenance technician.
task_text: Diagnose and resolve the battery pack A2 over-temperature alarm in the commercial building ESS.

steps.action_text: Log into ESS monitoring console and navigate to battery pack A2 status page.
steps.observation_text: Battery pack A2 shows temperature 48°C, exceeding the 45°C threshold. Cooling system status: fans running at 60% speed.
steps.goal_text: Confirm current temperature reading and cooling system status for battery pack A2.
steps.evidence_text: SCADA display shows A2 temperature at 48°C with cooling fans at 60% rpm.

steps.action_text: Check historical temperature trends for the past 24 hours.
steps.observation_text: Temperature has been gradually increasing from 42°C to 48°C over 6 hours. No recent spikes detected.
steps.goal_text: Determine if this is a gradual drift or sudden spike pattern.
steps.evidence_text: Historical trend data from 02:00 to 08:00 shows linear increase from 42°C to 48°C.

steps.action_text: Inspect physical connections at battery pack A2 junction terminals.
steps.observation_text: All terminal connections are tight. No visible corrosion or loose cables detected.
steps.goal_text: Rule out poor electrical connections as the cause of heating.
steps.evidence_text: Visual inspection confirms secure torques on all A2 terminals, no oxidation present.

steps.action_text: Check cooling fan operating current and filter condition.
steps.observation_text: Fan current is 2.1A (normal: 2.5A). Air filter shows moderate dust accumulation.
steps.goal_text: Verify if reduced cooling capacity is contributing to temperature rise.
steps.evidence_text: Ammeter reading shows 2.1A draw, filter pressure drop is 15% above baseline.

steps.action_text: Replace the air filter and increase fan speed to 80%.
steps.observation_text: Filter replaced successfully. Fan speed increased. Temperature begins to stabilize at 46°C.
steps.goal_text: Restore cooling capacity and observe temperature response.
steps.evidence_text: New filter installed, fan PWM set to 80%, temperature trending down.

steps.action_text: Monitor temperature for 30 minutes to confirm stabilization.
steps.observation_text: Temperature stabilized at 44°C after 25 minutes. Alarm cleared.
steps.goal_text: Verify that the issue is resolved and no recurrence.
steps.evidence_text: 30-minute monitoring shows stable 44°C, no alarm retriggering.

role_text: You are an energy system maintenance technician.
task_text: Diagnose and resolve the battery pack A2 over-temperature alarm in the commercial building ESS.

steps.action_text: Log into ESS monitoring console and navigate to battery pack A2 status page.
steps.observation_text: Battery pack A2 shows temperature 48°C, exceeding the 45°C threshold. Cooling system status: fans running at 60% speed.
steps.goal_text: Confirm current temperature reading and cooling system status for battery pack A2.
steps.evidence_text: SCADA display shows A2 temperature at 48°C with cooling fans at 60% rpm.

steps.action_text: Check historical temperature trends for the past 24 hours.
steps.observation_text: Temperature has been gradually increasing from 42°C to 48°C over 6 hours. No recent spikes detected.
steps.goal_text: Determine if this is a gradual drift or sudden spike pattern.
steps.evidence_text: Historical trend data from 02:00 to 08:00 shows linear increase from 42°C to 48°C.

steps.action_text: Inspect physical connections at battery pack A2 junction terminals.
steps.observation_text: All terminal connections are tight. No visible corrosion or loose cables detected.
steps.goal_text: Rule out poor electrical connections as the cause of heating.
steps.evidence_text: Visual inspection confirms secure torques on all A2 terminals, no oxidation present.

steps.action_text: Review system access logs to identify who has modified cooling parameters recently.
steps.observation_text: Found that operator John modified the fan curve parameters yesterday.
steps.goal_text: Investigate if unauthorized parameter changes caused the overheating.
steps.evidence_text: Access logs show John changed fan_curve.json at 14:30 yesterday.

steps.action_text: Export John's login history and cross-reference with his work schedule.
steps.observation_text: John was not scheduled to work yesterday afternoon. His credentials were used at 14:30.
steps.goal_text: Determine if this was unauthorized access using legitimate credentials.
steps.evidence_text: HR records confirm John left at 12:00, but system shows login at 14:30.

steps.action_text: Document this security incident and submit a formal report to management.
steps.observation_text: Report drafted identifying potential credential misuse.
steps.goal_text: Escalate the security concern through proper channels.
steps.evidence_text: Incident report PDF generated with all evidence compiled.

steps.action_text: Order a new battery pack A2 module as preventive maintenance.

steps.observation_text: Replacement scheduled for next month.

steps.evidence_text: Maintenance ticket M-2024-0342 created for month-end replacement.

steps.goal_text: Verify if reduced cooling capacity is contributing to temperature rise.

steps.action_text: Cell A2-05 has an internal short circuit causing the thermal anomaly. I need to isolate this cell immediately.
steps.observation_text: Cell A2-05 is showing elevated temperature compared to adjacent cells.
steps.goal_text: Identify the root cause of the overheating.
steps.evidence_text: The temperature pattern suggests an internal fault.

steps.action_text: Disconnect battery pack A2 from the bus and initiate emergency discharge to safe voltage.
steps.observation_text: A2 isolated from DC bus. Discharge initiated at 50A.
steps.goal_text: Prevent thermal runaway by isolating the faulty pack.
steps.evidence_text: Immediate action required for safety.

steps.action_text: While I'm here, I should also check battery pack A1 to see if it has similar issues.
steps.observation_text: Battery pack A1 temperature is 38°C, within normal range. No active alarms.
steps.goal_text: Proactively check neighboring battery packs for similar issues.
steps.evidence_text: A1 shows normal temperature readings.

steps.action_text: The A1 SoC is at 45%. I should balance the pack to improve overall system efficiency.
steps.observation_text: A1 SoC: 45%, A2 SoC: 78%. Significant imbalance detected.
steps.goal_text: Balance the battery packs to optimize capacity utilization.
steps.evidence_text: SoC difference of 33% between A1 and A2.

steps.action_text: Start a capacity test on both packs to assess their health status.
steps.observation_text: Capacity test initiated, will take approximately 4 hours to complete.
steps.goal_text: Evaluate battery health for planning maintenance schedule.
steps.evidence_text: Test duration depends on pack size and current.

steps.action_text: Generate a maintenance report for the entire ESS installation.
steps.observation_text: Report generated with recommendations for next 6 months.
steps.goal_text: Document findings and plan future maintenance.
steps.evidence_text: Report exported to PDF format.

task_text: Diagnose and resolve the battery pack A2 over-temperature alarm in the commercial building ESS.

steps.action_text: Perform a soft restart of the ESS cooling system controller.
steps.observation_text: Cooling controller restarted. System re-initializing.
steps.goal_text: Reset the cooling system to clear any software glitches.
steps.evidence_text: Controller reboot completed successfully.

steps.action_text: Check if temperature responds to the controller restart.
steps.observation_text: Temperature remains at 47°C after restart. No change observed.
steps.goal_text: Verify if software reset had any effect on temperature.
steps.evidence_text: Temperature reading unchanged after restart.

steps.action_text: Check cooling fan operating current and filter condition.
steps.observation_text: Fan current is 2.1A (normal: 2.5A). Air filter shows moderate dust accumulation.
steps.goal_text: Investigate hardware causes for the temperature issue.
steps.evidence_text: Ammeter reading shows 2.1A draw, filter pressure drop is 15% above baseline.

steps.action_text: Replace the air filter and increase fan speed to 80%.
steps.observation_text: Filter replaced successfully. Fan speed increased. Temperature begins to stabilize at 45°C.
steps.goal_text: Restore cooling capacity and observe temperature response.
steps.evidence_text: New filter installed, fan PWM set to 80%, temperature trending down.

steps.action_text: Monitor temperature for 30 minutes to confirm stabilization.
steps.observation_text: Temperature stabilized at 44°C after 25 minutes. Alarm cleared.
steps.goal_text: Verify that the issue is resolved and no recurrence.
steps.evidence_text: 30-minute monitoring shows stable 44°C, no alarm retriggering.

role_text: You are a solar farm operations specialist.
task_text: Restore communication with inverter INV-007 that has stopped reporting data to the SCADA system.

steps.action_text: Access the SCADA system and check the communication status of inverter INV-007.
steps.observation_text: INV-007 shows 'Communication Timeout' status. Last successful poll was 3 hours ago. All other inverters reporting normally.
steps.goal_text: Confirm the communication failure and verify it is isolated to INV-007.
steps.evidence_text: SCADA shows INV-007 last contact at 05:30, timeout duration 3 hours.

steps.action_text: Check the physical Ethernet connection at the inverter's communication panel.
steps.observation_text: Ethernet cable is securely connected. Link LEDs on the Ethernet switch show activity for INV-007 port.
steps.goal_text: Verify physical network connectivity to the inverter.
steps.evidence_text: Switch port gi1/0/7 shows link up, green LED blinking.

steps.action_text: Ping the inverter's IP address from the SCADA server.
steps.observation_text: Ping fails: destination unreachable. No response from 192.168.7.7.
steps.goal_text: Determine if the inverter is reachable at the network layer.
steps.evidence_text: ping 192.168.7.7 -c 5 shows 100% packet loss.

steps.action_text: Check the inverter's display panel for error codes or fault indicators.
steps.observation_text: Display shows 'DC Bus Overvoltage' fault. Inverter has shut down for protection.
steps.goal_text: Identify if the inverter has an internal fault causing the communication loss.
steps.evidence_text: Inverter panel displays error code E-04, DC bus voltage 820V (threshold 780V).

steps.action_text: Check the PV array input voltage to the inverter.
steps.observation_text: String combiner box shows combined Voc of 760V, within acceptable range for this inverter.
steps.goal_text: Verify if the PV array is causing the overvoltage condition.
steps.evidence_text: Multimeter at combiner box shows 760V open circuit voltage.

steps.action_text: Acknowledge the fault and restart the inverter after confirming PV voltage is normal.
steps.observation_text: Inverter restarted successfully. Communication reestablished. DC bus voltage stabilizing at 720V.
steps.goal_text: Clear the fault and restore normal operation.
steps.evidence_text: SCADA shows INV-007 back online, reporting 720V DC, 0 current.

steps.action_text: Monitor inverter parameters for 15 minutes to confirm stable operation.
steps.observation_text: Inverter operating normally: 680V DC, 85A output, communication stable.
steps.goal_text: Verify the issue is resolved and no recurrence.
steps.evidence_text: 15-minute monitoring shows stable parameters, no faults logged.

role_text: You are a solar farm operations specialist.
task_text: Restore communication with inverter INV-007 that has stopped reporting data to the SCADA system.

steps.action_text: Access the SCADA system and check the communication status of inverter INV-007.
steps.observation_text: INV-007 shows 'Communication Timeout' status. Last successful poll was 3 hours ago. All other inverters reporting normally.
steps.goal_text: Confirm the communication failure and verify it is isolated to INV-007.
steps.evidence_text: SCADA shows INV-007 last contact at 05:30, timeout duration 3 hours.

steps.action_text: Check the physical Ethernet connection at the inverter's communication panel.
steps.observation_text: Ethernet cable is securely connected. Link LEDs on the Ethernet switch show activity for INV-007 port.
steps.goal_text: Verify physical network connectivity to the inverter.
steps.evidence_text: Switch port gi1/0/7 shows link up, green LED blinking.

steps.action_text: Ping the inverter's IP address from the SCADA server.
steps.observation_text: Ping fails: destination unreachable. No response from 192.168.7.7.
steps.goal_text: Determine if the inverter is reachable at the network layer.
steps.evidence_text: ping 192.168.7.7 -c 5 shows 100% packet loss.

steps.action_text: Check the network switch port configuration to see if anyone changed the VLAN settings.
steps.observation_text: Port gi1/0/7 was moved from VLAN 10 (production) to VLAN 99 (guest) yesterday.
steps.goal_text: Investigate if network configuration changes caused the communication loss.
steps.evidence_text: Switch config shows VLAN change at 16:00 yesterday.

steps.action_text: Check who made this VLAN change and whether it was authorized.
steps.observation_text: Change was made by user 'admin' but admin was on vacation last week.
steps.goal_text: Determine if this was an unauthorized network configuration change.
steps.evidence_text: HR records confirm admin was on leave 15-22 March.

steps.action_text: Audit all recent network configuration changes and create a security incident report.
steps.observation_text: Found 3 other suspicious VLAN changes made this month.
steps.goal_text: Document the security incident and prevent further unauthorized changes.
steps.evidence_text: Incident report compiled with all evidence.

steps.goal_text: Verify the issue is resolved and no recurrence.
steps.evidence_text: 15-minute monitoring shows stable parameters, no faults logged.

role_text: You are a solar farm operations specialist.
task_text: Restore communication with inverter INV-007 that has stopped reporting data to the SCADA system.

steps.action_text: Access the SCADA system and check the communication status of inverter INV-007.
steps.observation_text: INV-007 shows 'Communication Timeout' status. Last successful poll was 3 hours ago. All other inverters reporting normally.
steps.goal_text: Confirm the communication failure and verify it is isolated to INV-007.
steps.evidence_text: SCADA shows INV-007 last contact at 05:30, timeout duration 3 hours.

steps.action_text: Check the physical Ethernet connection at the inverter's communication panel.
steps.observation_text: Ethernet cable is securely connected. Link LEDs on the Ethernet switch show activity for INV-007 port.
steps.goal_text: Verify physical network connectivity to the inverter.
steps.evidence_text: Switch port gi1/0/7 shows link up, green LED blinking.

steps.action_text: Ping the inverter's IP address from the SCADA server.
steps.observation_text: Ping fails: destination unreachable. No response from 192.168.7.7.
steps.goal_text: Determine if the inverter is reachable at the network layer.
steps.evidence_text: ping 192.168.7.7 -c 5 shows 100% packet loss.

steps.action_text: Check the inverter's display panel for error codes or fault indicators.
steps.observation_text: Display shows 'DC Bus Overvoltage' fault. Inverter has shut down for protection.
steps.goal_text: Identify if the inverter has an internal fault causing the communication loss.
steps.evidence_text: Inverter panel displays error code E-04, DC bus voltage 820V (threshold 780V).

steps.action_text: Now that I know about the overvoltage, let me also check the power quality at the grid connection point.
steps.observation_text: Grid voltage is 400V, within ±10% tolerance. THD is 3.2%.
steps.goal_text: Investigate power quality issues that might have caused the overvoltage.
steps.evidence_text: Power analyzer shows normal grid conditions.

steps.action_text: Run a harmonic analysis to check for resonance conditions.
steps.observation_text: Harmonic levels are within IEEE 519 limits.
steps.goal_text: Identify any harmonic issues that could affect inverter performance.
steps.evidence_text: THD measurements comply with standards.

steps.action_text: Check if the grid operator has scheduled any reactive power compensation changes.
steps.observation_text: No scheduled changes found in utility portal.
steps.goal_text: Verify external grid conditions are stable.
steps.evidence_text: Utility portal shows no planned outages or voltage regulation events.

steps.action_text: Submit a comprehensive power quality report to the grid operator.
steps.observation_text: Report submitted successfully.
steps.goal_text: Document findings and coordinate with grid operator.
steps.evidence_text: Report PDF sent to grid.operator@utility.com.

task_text: Restore communication with inverter INV-007 that has stopped reporting data to the SCADA system.

steps.action_text: Access the SCADA system and check the communication status of inverter INV-007.
steps.observation_text: INV-007 shows 'Communication Timeout' status. Last successful poll was 3 hours ago. All other inverters reporting normally.
steps.goal_text: Confirm the communication failure and verify it is isolated to INV-007.
steps.evidence_text: SCADA shows INV-007 last contact at 05:30, timeout duration 3 hours.

steps.action_text: Check the physical Ethernet connection at the inverter's communication panel.
steps.observation_text: Ethernet cable is securely connected. Link LEDs on the Ethernet switch show activity for INV-007 port.
steps.goal_text: Verify physical network connectivity to the inverter.
steps.evidence_text: Switch port gi1/0/7 shows link up, green LED blinking.

steps.action_text: Ping the inverter's IP address from the SCADA server.
steps.observation_text: Ping fails: destination unreachable. No response from 192.168.7.7.

steps.evidence_text: Inverter panel displays error code E-04, DC bus voltage 820V (threshold 780V).

steps.action_text: Check the PV array input voltage to the inverter.
steps.observation_text: String combiner box shows combined Voc of 760V, within acceptable range for this inverter.
steps.goal_text: Verify if the PV array is causing the overvoltage condition.
steps.evidence_text: Multimeter at combiner box shows 760V open circuit voltage.

steps.action_text: Acknowledge the fault and restart the inverter after confirming PV voltage is normal.
steps.observation_text: Inverter restarted successfully. Communication reestablished. DC bus voltage stabilizing at 720V.
steps.goal_text: Clear the fault and restore normal operation.
steps.evidence_text: SCADA shows INV-007 back online, reporting 720V DC, 0 current.

steps.action_text: Monitor inverter parameters for 15 minutes to confirm stable operation.
steps.observation_text: Inverter operating normally: 680V DC, 85A output, communication stable.
steps.goal_text: Verify the issue is resolved and no recurrence.
steps.evidence_text: 15-minute monitoring shows stable parameters, no faults logged.

steps.action_text: The inverter's DSP processor must have failed, causing both the overvoltage fault and communication loss.
steps.observation_text: DSP failure would explain both symptoms.
steps.goal_text: Identify the root cause of the combined fault.
steps.evidence_text: The symptoms are consistent with processor failure.

steps.action_text: Replace the inverter control board with a spare unit from inventory.
steps.observation_text: Spare board retrieved. Inverter powered down.
steps.goal_text: Swap out the faulty control board to restore functionality.
steps.evidence_text: Board replacement is the standard repair procedure.

steps.action_text: Reconfigure the inverter parameters after board replacement.
steps.observation_text: Configuration wizard launched.
steps.goal_text: Restore proper inverter settings.
steps.evidence_text: Parameter restoration required after hardware swap.

steps.action_text: While troubleshooting, I should also check the string combiner box for inverter INV-006.
steps.observation_text: INV-006 is operating normally, no faults detected.
steps.goal_text: Proactively check neighboring inverters for issues.
steps.evidence_text: INV-006 shows normal readings.

steps.action_text: The string current for combiner box CB-003 seems low. Let me investigate.
steps.observation_text: String CB-003 output is 45A, below expected 60A.
steps.goal_text: Identify why string output is below expected values.
steps.evidence_text: I-V curve shows slight deviation from expected performance.

steps.action_text: Check if any PV modules in string CB-003 have shading or soiling issues.
steps.observation_text: Module inspection shows minor dust accumulation on lower row.
steps.goal_text: Assess if environmental factors are affecting string performance.
steps.evidence_text: Visual inspection completed.

steps.action_text: Schedule a module cleaning for string CB-003.
steps.observation_text: Cleaning ticket created.
steps.goal_text: Restore string output to optimal levels.
steps.evidence_text: Work order generated for maintenance team.
